name: Thai Soy Bean Fritters
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Kaffir lime peel or lime zest
1 3/4 cups Cooked and pureed soy beans
1 tsp Finely chopped Kaffir lime leaves
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the dried chilies in hot water until soft. Drain and discard any tough stems.
2. In a mortar and pestle or blender, puree the soaked chilies, coriander roots, Galanga, lime zest, garlic, onions, and shrimp paste. Add the cooked soy beans and rice flour; blend well.
3. Transfer to a bowl and stir in the eggs, Kaffir lime leaves, cilantro, and fish sauce until combined.
4. Heat oil in a large flat-bottom pan over medium heat.
5. Using wet hands, form the batter into patties about 1-1/2 inches in diameter.
6. Fry the patties until golden brown on all sides. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
7. Serve hot with the dipping sauce.
Dipping Sauce:
1. In a small saucepan, heat salt, sugar, and vinegar over low heat until dissolved.
2. Cool slightly, then stir in the minced chilies until well combined.
3. The sauce should be on the dry side to ensure crispy fritters when fried. If needed, let patties air dry for 15 minutes before frying.
